title: Bee Boat and Spirit World
summary: A spirit-world passage involving a boat made of bees and a return from a much longer absence than expected.
excerpt: # Bee Boat and Spirit World After fleeing the tendril creature, the cabal is pushed into the spirit world. There they encounter a boat made of bees, not giant bees but a mass of ordinary tiny bees forming a vessel. The bee boat carries the cabal through a symbolic and spiritual passage tied to the c

title: Caleb
summary: A member of the Riverbed Boys, practical and wary when the cabal asks about Paige.
excerpt: # Caleb Caleb is one of the [[Riverbed Boys]] at [[Saltwind Line]]. He is part of the musical circle that speaks with the cabal about [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]. He is careful about what he says, especially when the conversation moves from gossip into blame, old trials, and the secrets of the [[Salton S

title: Campaign Overview
summary: Sands Under the Slab is a Mage: The Ascension chronicle about new mages, Slab City, a dead librarian, and a prison under the Salton Sea.
excerpt: # Campaign Overview Sands Under the Slab is a Mage: The Ascension chronicle set around [[Slab City]], [[Salton Sea]], and the strange magical ecology of the desert. The player characters arrive as recent outsiders and half-initiates. Most have been in the area only a few days when a sandstorm drives

title: Dry Bones
summary: A desert death and paradox spirit in a duster and hat, enforcing the dry law of Slab City.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Dry Bones Dry Bones is a paradox or desert-death spirit associated with thirst, bones, exposure, and the desert's refusal to be made lush by careless magic. He appears as a skeletal or deathly figure in a duster and hat. The cabal sees him after the time vision connected to [[East Jesus]] and the

title: East Jesus
summary: An art-installation site where the Gauntlet is thin and a broken TV opens onto a vision of older sacrifices and tendril creatures.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # East Jesus East Jesus is an art-installation site in Slab City. The Gauntlet is especially low here, and the place is thick with strange resonance. During the cabal's investigation, a broken television becomes a focal point for a vision or spirit event tied to the [[Tendril Creatures]]. ## Known E

title: Eli
summary: Head of The Beat, an early guide to Slab City's rules and a politically important local mage.
excerpt: # Eli Eli is the head of [[The Beat]]. He appears early as someone who can explain Slab City's rules to new arrivals and later speaks with [[Razor Tom]] at [[Music Camp]]. He is helpful but not necessarily trusted by the cabal. As the mystery deepens, the characters wonder who among the local leader

title: Gold and Quintessence
summary: A recurring clue tying sacrifice, tendril creatures, fertility, and visions of the past together.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Gold and Quintessence Gold and quintessence appear together in the visions and aftermath of the [[Tendril Creatures]]. At [[East Jesus]], Ian coughs up gold and water after contact with the vision. In the past, sacrifice at the [[Temple of the Sun and Moon]] releases power that feeds the creature.

title: Gragnock Stoneheart
summary: A local rock spirit whose true name appears in Paige's library tome and becomes a powerful instrument for Ian.
excerpt: # Gragnock Stoneheart Gragnock Stoneheart is a local rock spirit named in a tome from [[The Library]]. Ian learns the name while reading Paige's spirit book. Speaking or invoking the name appears to matter magically and may count as a unique instrument for spirit work. ## Known Connections - [[Ian]]

title: Ian
summary: A newly arrived mage whose avatar is bound up with a rock, a spirit tome, and the name Gragnock Stoneheart.
excerpt: # Ian Ian is one of the new mages gathered by the storm at [[The Library]]. He becomes closely tied to the campaign's spirit mysteries through a tome, a rock, and the name [[Gragnock Stoneheart]]. ## Known Events - Reads a spirit tome from the Library. - Learns the name [[Gragnock Stoneheart]], a lo

title: Keith
summary: Paige's nephew, pulled into Slab City by his aunt's death and the legacy she left behind.
excerpt: # Keith Keith is [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]'s nephew. He arrives in Slab City shortly before the chronicle begins and is drawn into the mystery when Paige's body is found inside [[The Library]]. Paige appears to have made Keith's journey to Slab City easier and may have arranged circumstances so he woul

title: Keith's Aunt Paige
summary: The dead librarian whose murder opens the chronicle; brilliant, protective, feared, and accused of dangerous mind, time, and spirit magic.
excerpt: # Keith's Aunt Paige Paige was Keith's aunt and the keeper of [[The Library]]. She had lived in Slab City for years and gave shelter to people who needed a place to recover, learn, or hide. She is found dead at the start of the chronicle. Her death becomes the cabal's first mystery. ## Reputation Pa

title: Mouse
summary: A Shattered Bottles member who reportedly defended Paige during a local accusation or trial.
excerpt: # Mouse Mouse is a member of [[Shattered Bottles]] and reportedly served as [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]'s defense during a local accusation or trial. The cabal has not yet fully questioned Mouse, making them a key person of interest for understanding what Paige was accused of, who judged her, and why she

title: Music Camp
summary: A social and magical gathering place where music, memory, performance, and tribe politics overlap.
excerpt: # Music Camp Music Camp is a gathering place of roughly a few dozen people, makeshift speakers, informal stage space, and strong social magic. The cabal arrives near sunset after the events at [[East Jesus]]. Music Camp becomes a place to recover, learn local politics, and encounter the factions tha

title: Nina
summary: A Riverbed Boys member who once stayed at Paige's Library for months and still carries gratitude and guarded knowledge.
excerpt: # Nina Nina is a member of the [[Riverbed Boys]]. When she first came to Slab City, [[Keith's Aunt Paige]] let her stay at [[The Library]] for four or five months while she got her bearings. Nina's gratitude does not mean she thinks Paige was harmless. Like the rest of the Riverbed Boys, she carries

title: Old Man Hummingbird
summary: A Riverbed Boys elder whose music and spirit knowledge guide the cabal toward the Salton Sea.
excerpt: # Old Man Hummingbird Old Man Hummingbird is one of the [[Riverbed Boys]]. He carries deep spiritual knowledge and speaks through music as much as through explanation. He recognizes the exchange of life, memory, and future in the cabal's interactions. His music can make the path of a timeline feel l

title: Open Quests
summary: A practical tracker for the campaign's unresolved mysteries and next actions.
excerpt: # Open Quests ## Investigate Paige's Murder Find who killed [[Keith's Aunt Paige]], what was taken from [[The Library]], and whether the killing was punishment, cover-up, mercy, or containment. ## Question Mouse [[Mouse]] reportedly defended Paige during a local accusation or trial. They may know wh

title: Order of Reason
summary: Historical predecessors or analogues of the Union seen in the temple vision, tied to conquistadors and the breaking of an older ritual system.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Order of Reason The Order of Reason appears through the [[East Jesus]] time vision and the [[Temple of the Sun and Moon]]. The cabal sees conquistador or proto-Technocratic figures interacting with a system that had been used to sedate and feed a [[Tendril Creatures|tendril creature]]. ## Signific

title: Player Characters
summary: Working index of the cabal members named in the early Sands Under the Slab transcripts.
excerpt: # Player Characters This is the working player-character index for the early Sands Under the Slab transcripts. ## Named Pages - [[Keith]] - [[Ian]] - [[Twilight]] ## Additional Cabal Members Mentioned - Mark, whose time magic helps trigger or deepen the [[East Jesus]] vision. - Melina or Lena, assoc

title: Pompey
summary: A name connected to Paige's earlier trouble and possible defense or protection in Slab City politics.
excerpt: # Pompey Pompey is mentioned in connection with [[Keith's Aunt Paige]] and earlier Slab City trouble. The current understanding is that Pompey may have helped Paige survive or get clear of a prior accusation before the final events that led to her death. ## Open Questions - Who is Pompey in Slab Cit

title: Razor Tom
summary: A Shattered Bottles member who speaks with Eli and points the cabal toward Salvation Mountain.
excerpt: # Razor Tom Razor Tom is a member of [[Shattered Bottles]]. The cabal sees him speaking with [[Eli]] at [[Music Camp]]. He talks about [[Salvation Mountain]], the devotion that built it, and the way repeated love or hunger can make a place spiritually significant. ## Connections - [[Shattered Bottle

title: Riverbed Boys
summary: A circle of Slab City mages tied to music, shared memory, and Paige's past.
excerpt: # Riverbed Boys The Riverbed Boys are a circle of mages living around [[Saltwind Line]]. They are known as allies or at least consistent friends of [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]. Their magic is expressed through music, harmony, shared attention, and memory. When they play together, the experience can carry

title: Salton Sea
summary: The toxic sea at the center of the campaign's largest secret: a Union-made or Union-maintained prison that bleeds the land.
excerpt: # Salton Sea The Salton Sea is not only a polluted body of water. It is the visible face of a deeper imprisonment, warding, and ecological wound. As the cabal approaches, the Gauntlet rises. The spiritual world tightens and resists. Old Man Hummingbird identifies this as the place where [[The Union]

title: Salton Sea Prison
summary: The central magical problem: a prison beneath or around the Salton Sea that contains dangerous spirit-creatures by bleeding the land dry.
excerpt: # Salton Sea Prison The Salton Sea Prison is the largest known mystery in the chronicle. It appears to be a magical and possibly Technocratic containment system beneath or around the [[Salton Sea]]. The prison contains one or more [[Tendril Creatures]]. It may keep them trapped by draining fertility

title: Saltwind Line
summary: A settlement of dead pads, dugouts, and cool underground places where the Riverbed Boys live and play.
excerpt: # Saltwind Line Saltwind Line is a place of dead pads, old trailer beds, improvised hovels, and dugout spaces cut into the desert. Beneath some of the structures are cooler, moist rooms that feel almost impossible compared to the heat outside. The cabal comes here to speak to the [[Riverbed Boys]],

title: Salvation Mountain
summary: A node and spiritual landmark tied to love, hunger, local politics, and the attacks by a younger tendril creature.
excerpt: # Salvation Mountain Salvation Mountain is a major spiritual landmark and node. [[Razor Tom]] speaks of it as a place shaped by repeated devotion, love, and the builder's overwhelming focus. The cabal learns that Salvation Mountain may be one of the easiest ways to tap into time and place, but it is

title: Session 00 - Prelude
summary: The characters shelter in the Library during a sandstorm and discover Paige's body.
excerpt: # Session 00 - Prelude The chronicle opens in June heat. The characters have been in [[Slab City]] for only a few days when a sandstorm pushes them toward [[The Library]]. Inside, they meet one another, shelter from the storm, and begin to see how strange the local community is. Bees, cobras, patche

title: Session 01 - Official Game One
summary: The cabal wakes into Slab City, reads Paige's tome, learns Gragnock's name, and begins meeting the local mage community.
excerpt: # Session 01 - Official Game One The first official session begins after the discovery of Paige's body. Some characters have stayed at [[The Library]], and the cabal starts the day trying to understand what the place is and how Slab City works. Ian reads from a tome connected to Paige and learns the

title: Session 02 - The Last Cup and East Jesus
summary: The cabal questions Tasman Quill, learns more about the Union, and triggers a dangerous vision at East Jesus.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Session 02 - The Last Cup and East Jesus The cabal visits [[The Last Cup]] and speaks with [[Tasman Quill]], an ex-Syndicate mage with a Technocratic artificial arm. Tasman explains pieces of [[The Union]] and pushes back against the idea that she is still acting as one of them. The group then inv

title: Session 03 - Music Camp and Salvation Mountain
summary: Music Camp gives the cabal faction leads, then the route toward Salvation Mountain turns into a tendril-creature attack.
excerpt: # Session 03 - Music Camp and Salvation Mountain After the East Jesus vision, the cabal heads to [[Music Camp]] near sunset. Twilight performs a shared-experience set that energizes the crowd and gives him unexpected quintessence. The cabal meets or observes [[Eli]] and [[Razor Tom]]. Razor Tom spea

title: Session 04 - Spirit World and Time Vision
summary: The cabal flees into the spirit world, rides the bee boat, witnesses older temple sacrifices, and returns after a long absence.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Session 04 - Spirit World and Time Vision Fleeing the tendril creature, the cabal enters the spirit world. The desert becomes wet, salty, and strange. A [[Bee Boat and Spirit World|boat made of bees]] carries them through the experience. The cabal witnesses or inhabits scenes around the [[Temple o

title: Session 05 - Riverbed Boys and the Salton Sea
summary: The Riverbed Boys reveal more about Paige, then guide the cabal toward the prison under the Salton Sea.
excerpt: # Session 05 - Riverbed Boys and the Salton Sea The cabal goes to [[Saltwind Line]] to speak with the [[Riverbed Boys]], who were known allies of [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]. Caleb, Nina, and Old Man Hummingbird reveal that Paige helped people but also pushed dangerous boundaries. She was accused of mani

title: Shattered Bottles
summary: A Slab City faction connected to Razor Tom and Mouse.
excerpt: # Shattered Bottles Shattered Bottles is one of the known Slab City factions or tribes. [[Razor Tom]] is a member. [[Mouse]], who reportedly defended [[Keith's Aunt Paige]], is also connected to the group. ## Known Connections - [[Razor Tom]] - [[Mouse]] - [[The Beat]] - [[Keith's Aunt Paige]]

title: Slab City
summary: A desert community and mage refuge where survival, secrecy, and local spiritual law matter as much as faction politics.
excerpt: # Slab City Slab City is the desert refuge where the chronicle begins. In June the heat sits around 110 degrees or worse, so even ordinary social movement becomes difficult and dangerous. For mages, Slab City is both shelter and trap. The Gauntlet is strange, the desert has opinions, and the communi

title: Slab City Mage Law
summary: The informal rules, taboos, and survival logic that govern mage life in Slab City.
excerpt: # Slab City Mage Law Slab City mage law is not written as a neat code. It is carried in warnings, stories, faction pressure, spiritual consequences, and memories of what happened last time someone pushed too hard. ## Known Principles - Do not attract [[The Union]]. - Do not create a magical crisis o

title: Tasman Quill
summary: The ex-Syndicate owner of the Last Cup, still carrying a Technocratic artificial arm and a hard-earned distrust of the Union.
excerpt: # Tasman Quill Tasman Quill runs [[The Last Cup]]. She is an ex-Technocrat, specifically tied to the Syndicate, and still carries obvious signs of that past, including a Union-made artificial arm. She is wary, practical, and not interested in being treated as an active enemy. When challenged about w

title: Temple of the Sun and Moon
summary: A vision-location from the past where a tendril creature was sedated and ritually fed through sacrifice.
excerpt: # Temple of the Sun and Moon The Temple of the Sun and Moon is a past vision seen through the events at [[East Jesus]]. In the vision, a tendril creature is held, sedated, and ritually fed. On some days the Temple of the Sun holds the creature; on other days the Temple of the Moon does. The system s

title: Tendril Creatures
summary: Powerful tentacled spirit-creatures tied to gold, quintessence, sacrifice, and the prison beneath the Salton Sea.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
excerpt: # Tendril Creatures The Tendril Creatures are large, tentacled or tendriled beings connected to quintessence, gold, fertility, and sacrifice. The cabal first encounters signs of them through [[East Jesus]], then through visions of older rituals and later direct attacks near [[Salvation Mountain]]. #

title: The Beat
summary: A Slab City faction or tribe led by Eli, tied to music, local rules, and social authority.
excerpt: # The Beat The Beat is one of the local groups or tribes of Slab City. [[Eli]] is identified as its head. The Beat is tied to music, social coordination, and the local rules that allow mages to survive without bringing disaster on everyone. ## Known Connections - [[Eli]] - [[Music Camp]] - [[Slab Ci

title: The Last Cup
summary: A coffee stop and information hub run by ex-Syndicate mage Tasman Quill.
excerpt: # The Last Cup The Last Cup is a coffee place and social hub in Slab City, run by [[Tasman Quill]]. It is a place where tourists, drifters, and local mages can intersect without everyone admitting what they are. The cabal comes here early while trying to understand the rules of Slab City and the dan

title: The Library
summary: Paige's library, a protected anchor stone where the cabal first gathers and where Paige's body is discovered.
excerpt: # The Library The Library is the first sanctuary of the chronicle. During the opening sandstorm, the player characters gather here for shelter and discover that [[Keith's Aunt Paige]], the woman who maintained the place, has been killed. The building is more than a collection of books. It has perman

title: The Union
summary: The Technocratic Union as understood in Slab City: systems, control, drones, sacrifice, and abandoned problems.
excerpt: # The Union The Union is the local name used for the Technocratic Union. In the early sessions, the cabal learns about it through [[Tasman Quill]], local warnings, and the history of the [[Salton Sea Prison]]. The Union believes it knows how reality should be organized. In Slab City terms, that mean

title: Timeline
summary: Chronological outline of the early Sands Under the Slab campaign.
links: Temple of the Sun and Moon
Session 02 - The Last Cup and East Jesus
Session 03 - Music Camp and Salvation Mountain
Session 04 - Spirit World and Time Vision
Session 05 - Riverbed Boys and the Salton Sea
excerpt: # Timeline ## Before The Campaign - The [[Tendril Creatures]] are once managed through sacrificial rites at the [[Temple of the Sun and Moon]]. - The old system is damaged or broken after outside interference and overfeeding. - [[The Union]] later creates or maintains the [[Salton Sea Prison]]. - [[

title: Twilight
summary: A drug sherpa, performer, and shared-experience mage whose methods fit Slab City's strange social magic.
excerpt: # Twilight Twilight is one of the player characters and a natural fit for Slab City's improvised mystic culture. He acts as a drug sherpa for tourists and seekers, guiding experiences rather than merely selling substances. ## Known Style - Uses altered states, performance, recordings, and shared exp
